channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Prices have leaked for the Google Pixel 10 series – as well as several generations of the Pro Fold
item.description: Your next Pixel phone could cost either more or less than the current models, depending on which you choose.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Five things to consider before you deploy an LLM
item.description: If the screwdriver were invented by the tech industry today, then it would be widely deployed for a variety of tasks, including hammering nails. Since the debut of ChatGPT, there has been growing fervor and backlash against large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What is Kubernetes? Scalable cloud-native applications
item.description: Kubernetes is a popular open source platform for container orchestration—that is, for managing applications built from multiple, largely self-contained runtimes called containers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: From training dogs to intelligent machines: Here’s how reinforcement learning is teaching AI
item.description: Understanding intelligence and creating intelligent machines are grand scientific challenges of our times. The ability to learn from experience is a cornerstone of intelligence for machines and living beings alike.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: This trick could allow you to upgrade an AMD RX 9070 GPU to be an RX 9070 XT for free, in theory – but I’d seriously advise against it
item.description: Can an AMD RX 9070 GPU equal an RX 9070 XT? Yes, it sounds preposterous, but it’s (kind of) true – if these modders are to be believed.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: New White House directive lifts AI safeguards in the name of modernization
item.description: Trump administration wants quicker and better AI amid ongoing trade wars, but safety and clear guidance remain paramount.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Ancient flaw that allowed hackers to view your Chrome browser history has finally been patched, so update now
item.description: Google Chrome is finally fixing the clicked links color issue which troubled browser makers for decades.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Musk calls Navarro 'moron' after 'demonstrably false' claims about Tesla
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk slammed President Trump's trade adviser Peter Navarro on Tuesday, calling him "truly a moron" in their latest spat over the administration's new import taxes. "Navarro is truly a moron.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Audio-Technica’s limited-edition $199 gold turntable is the reason I’ll be waiting in line at 8am on Record Store Day
item.description: Audio-Technica’s limited AT-LP60XBT turntable in gold is an independent in-store exclusive, and if I don't manage to get one, my Record Store Day will be ruined.
item.dc:creator: becky.scarrott@futurenet.com (Becky Scarrott), TechRadar

item.title: Prague Zoo Joins Global Effort to Save Rare Insect
item.description: The Prague Zoo has joined an international effort to ensure the survival of a rare insect considered extinct for over 80 years.
item.dc:creator: Karel Janicek, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 7 risk management rules every CIO should follow
item.description: Risk is inescapable. Look around and you’ll see technological, economic, and competitive obstacles that CIOs must not only handle, but defeat.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Decision-making 101: How to get consensus right
item.description: IT leaders have five ways to make decisions. Their decision-making can be (1) Authoritarian; (2) Consultative; (3) through Delegation; (4) by submitting the alternatives to a Vote; or (5) by bringing the group to Consensus.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A US-Russian crew of 3 arrives at the International Space Station
item.description: NASA astronaut Jonny Kim and two Russian crewmates have arrived at the International Space Station on board a Russian spacecraft.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Europe considers new tariffs that could punish tech companies like Google, Meta, and Apple
item.description: As the European Union looks at how best to respond to Donald Trump’s trade war, officials are considering further escalating things, taking them to a point where it could really hurt America’s Big Tech companies.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cloudflare unveils agentic AI development tools
item.description: Following on its February release of the Agents SDK JavaScript framework for building AI agents, Cloudflare began its Developer Week today by announcing a series of tools and features designed to accelerate agentic AI development.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Kotlin, Swift, and Ruby losing popularity – Tiobe index
item.description: The Kotlin, Swift, and Ruby languages have slipped from their top 20 positions in the Tiobe index of programming language popularity. All three seem to have lost traction and to be in decline, according to Tiobe CEO Paul Jansen.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 9 steps to take to prepare for a quantum future
item.description: Over the past year, vendor after vendor has reached the critical quantum-computing milestone where adding more qubits no longer adds a disproportionately higher amount of errors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: GOP rep says Russia and China are 'laughing at us' after NSA director firing
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon (R-Neb.), a moderate Republican, slammed President Trump’s decision to fire the director of the National Security Agency (NSA) and his top deputy after demands from far-right influencer Laura Loomer, saying Sunday that China and Russia are “laughing at us” after that decision.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Apple just made it much easier to get a handle on your inbox. Here’s how
item.description: Keeping our inboxes organized often feels like an overwhelming task. If you’re fortunate, yours contains only messages from people you wish to communicate with. Realistically, though, most are cluttered with newsletters, receipts, social media digests, and more.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: 'Battlestar Galactica' is 'even more relevant now,' star says
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How internet search algorithms could help critical infrastructure
item.description: Researchers found the algorithms that rank pages in internet searches also can help planners understand how to prevent failures in electrical systems. The post How internet search algorithms could help critical infrastructure first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Relentless hackers abandon Windows to target your Apple ID
item.description: Experts say hackers who used to focus on Windows operating systems are increasingly targeting Apple IDs as part of a new phishing campaign.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Humanoid robot stuns with perfect side-flip acrobatics
item.description: Unitree, a Chinese robotics company that developed a backflipping robot, has now introduced a humanoid robot capable of doing a side flip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New MLCommons benchmarks to test AI infrastructure performance
item.description: Industry consortium MLCommons has released new versions of its MLPerf Inference benchmarks, offering a closer look at how current-generation data center and edge hardware performs under increasingly demanding AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: United receives FAA certification on Starlink-equipped aircraft
item.description: United has received FAA approval for a Starlink-equipped plane. The first commercial flight is set for May.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Leadership, trust and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare
item.description: As the military navigates the complexities of the 21st-century battlefield, the integration of artificial intelligence is a leadership imperative. The post Leadership, trust and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Why no one is challenging Trump's executive order that keeps TikTok running
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After TikTok was banned in the United States earlier this year, President Donald Trump gave the platform a reprieve, barreling past a law that was passed in Congress and upheld unanimously by the Supreme Court that said the ban was necessary for national security.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
